ground_truth.title: Entry Level Accountant Academy (7 CPE Credits)
ground_truth.main_content:
Entry-Level Accountant Academy - Learn How To Perform The Essential Tasks is a practical online course for anyone starting a career in accounting. In just 4.5 hours of video lessons across 3 modules, you'll learn to apply the Accounting equation, record revenues and expenses under US GAAP, and make end-of-period adjustments with confidence.

You’ll also master the Accounts Payable process—from invoice approval to payments—and get hands-on with QuickBooks and automation tools to streamline workflows.

By the end of the course, you’ll be equipped with real-world, job-ready accounting skills that employers value—perfect for students, new hires, or career changers.

After completing this course, the learner should be able to:

- Define the Accounting Equation and apply it to the various types of business transactions
- Apply US GAAP principles to revenues and expenses recording
- Identify revenue and costs adjustments at the end of the financial period
- Distinguish between all tasks in Accounts Payable – including vendor invoice recording, approval, and payment processes in an accounts payable system.
- Apply Accounting rules in the implementation of systems (QuickBooks) to streamline the financial processes

Course Details

Duration: 4.5 hours

Access Duration: Lifetime

Field of Study: Accounting

CPE Credits - NASBA: 7

Knowledge Level: Basic

Delivery Method: QAS Self-Study (on-demand video lectures)

Prerequisites: High school degree or equivalent

Advanced Prep: None

Controller Academy LLC is registered with the National Association of  State Boards of Accountancy (NASBA) as a sponsor of continuing professional education on the National Registry of CPE Sponsors. State  boards of accountancy have final authority on the acceptance of individual  courses for CPE credit. Complaints regarding registered sponsors may be  submitted to the National Registry of CPE Sponsors through its web site:  www.nasbaregistry.org

Curriculum

The Course Structure

Module 1: The Basics of Accounting
35 Minutes, 9 lectures, 3 quizzes
Covers the basics of accounting, double-entry system, debits and credits using DEALER and equation methods, the flow of accounting data, creation of financial statements, and ethics in accounting.

Module 2: Recording Revenue & Expense Transactions
2 hours, 20 lectures, 6 Excel templates, 3 quizzes
Focuses on practical setup and recording of transactions—billing, invoicing, payroll, vendor payments, and expense tracking—with real-world tools and accounting impact for each step.

Module 3: Period-end Close
1.7 hours, 12 lectures, 4 Excel templates, 6 quizzes
Explains the full period-end closing process—revenue and expense accruals, reconciliations, adjustments, and finalizing the balance sheet to ensure accurate financial reporting.
